docs.title: What is mockr?
docs.text:
mockr is a CLI tool that spins up a fake REST API from a JSON config file. no backend, no setup — define your endpoints and start building.

useful when your backend isn't ready yet, or you just want to prototype something fast without spinning up a real server.

  • one JSON file, one command
  • custom status codes and response delays
  • auto-reload with watch mode
  • CORS enabled by default
  • every request logged to the terminal

No install needed

just run npx mockr-cli start in any folder with an api.json.

$ npx mockr-cli start

  mockr running on http://localhost:3000

  GET     /users
  POST    /login

docs.title: api.json format
docs.text:
Each key is a route in the format METHOD /path, each value is the response body.

{
  "GET /users": [...],
  "GET /users/1": {...},
  "POST /login": {...},
  "DELETE /users/1": { "success": true }
}

Supported methods: GET POST PUT PATCH DELETE

Note

The config file defaults to api.json in the current directory. Use --config to point to a different file.

docs.title: CLI options
docs.text:
All flags are optional.

Flag Default Description --config <file> api.json path to your config file --port <number> 3000 port to run the server on --watch off auto-reload when config changes 
mockr-cli start --config mocks/api.json --port 8080 --watch

docs.title: Response delays
docs.text:
Simulate slow networks using _delay (value in milliseconds).

{
  "GET /users": { "_delay": 1500, "data": [{ "id": 1, "name": "Alice" }] }
}

Combine with _status to simulate a slow error:

{
  "GET /slow-error": { "_delay": 2000, "_status": 503, "error": "Service Unavailable" }
}

docs.title: Use without installing
docs.text:
The easiest way — no install needed:

npx mockr-cli start

docs.title: Install globally
docs.text:
Install once and run from anywhere:

npm install -g mockr-cli

Once installed, run mockr-cli start from any folder.

docs.title: Request logging
docs.text:
Every request is logged to the terminal automatically — no config needed.

  10:32:01  200  GET /users
  10:32:04  404  GET /unknown
  10:32:09  200  GET /users  (1500ms delay)
  10:32:11  403  GET /secret

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text: Up and running in under a minute.

docs.title: 2. Start mockr
docs.text:
npx mockr-cli start

  mockr running on http://localhost:3000

  GET     /users
  POST    /login

docs.title: Status codes
docs.text:
Return any HTTP status code using the _status field.

{
  "GET /secret": { "_status": 403, "error": "Forbidden" },
  "GET /crash":  { "_status": 500, "error": "Internal Server Error" }
}

Note

Fields starting with _ are reserved for mockr and won't appear in the response body.

docs.title: Watch mode
docs.text:
Pass --watch and mockr reloads automatically whenever you save your config file. No restart needed.

mockr-cli start --watch

  mockr running on http://localhost:3000
  watching api.json for changes...

  [reload] api.json changed
